message:
fix(security): harden DGIS topology risk aggregation against NaN/Infinity inputs

update_copilot.rs:
- Add normalize_unit_metric() helper that treats non-finite values
  (NaN, Infinity, -Infinity) as maximum risk (1.0) via fail-closed
  semantics, then clamps the result to [0.0, 1.0]
- Apply normalize_unit_metric() to fan_out_norm, betweenness_centrality,
  trust_bottleneck_score, and the final aggregate_risk() output in
  TopologyRiskMetrics::aggregate_risk()
- Previously, NaN inputs would silently propagate through the weighted
  sum, potentially producing NaN aggregate scores that bypass risk
  thresholds; now they are clamped to maximum risk
- Negative metric values are now clamped to 0.0 floor instead of
  passing through unchecked

Add 3 new tests:
- aggregate_risk_clamps_negative_metrics_to_zero_floor: verifies
  all-negative inputs produce 0.0 aggregate risk
- aggregate_risk_non_finite_metrics_fail_closed_to_maximum: verifies
  NaN/Infinity inputs produce 1.0 aggregate risk
- non_finite_metrics_do_not_suppress_high_risk_classification: verifies
  that corrupted topology telemetry triggers Critical risk level and
  requires operator acknowledgement in the full UpdateCopilot pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
